segments.s722-g105: فراوان is an adverb/adjectival quantifier placed before the noun phrase to indicate abundance ('many' or 'plentifully'). فرستاده را uses the object/topic marker را to mark 'the envoy' as the affected party (here the recipient), which in classical/poetic Persian can stand before the verb. هدیه is the direct object 'gift(s)' and داد is the simple past 'gave.' Together هدیه داد = 'gave (a) gift(s)'; with فراوان it conveys 'gave many gifts' or 'gave abundantly to the envoy.'
segments.s722-g106: جهاندیده is a compound adjective/noun 'world‑wise, experienced' serving as the subject (here referring back to the envoy). از مرو is a prepositional phrase 'from Merv' with از marking origin. برگشت is the past tense verb 'returned.' شاد is a predicative adjective 'happy/joyful' following the verb and describing the subject's state on returning (i.e. 'returned rejoicing'). The sequence subject + از phrase + verb + adjective is a natural poetic word order.
segments.s722-g57: باد here is the optative particle meaning "may (it be)"; the clause reads literally "May this great reward be to you." شما را uses را with a beneficiary sense (marking the person who receives the blessing). بدین مزد بسیار is a compact phrase: بدین (به + این, “to/with this”) + مزد ('reward') + بسیار ('very/large'), so the word order places the demonstrative phrase before the noun it modifies.
segments.s722-g58: ورا is a dative/benefactive pronoun "to him/her." داور دادگر یار باد is an optative clause: "may the just/judging arbiter be (his) ally." دادگر functions adjectivally with داور ('the just judge'), یار is predicate noun 'ally', and باد again marks the wish.
segments.s722-g59: یکی ناگهان is an adverbial sequence meaning roughly "one moment / suddenly." مرگ بود is simple past copula "there was a death." آن نه خرد is a parenthetical comment: آن ('that') + نه ('not') + خرد ('wisdom'), i.e. "that was not wisdom/foresight." Word order places the negated noun phrase after the copula for emphasis.
segments.s722-g60: که introduces a subordinate clause explaining or consequence-marking the previous statement ('so that' / 'such that'). کس here means 'no one' in combination with the negative verb that follows. در جهان is locative 'in the world,' ز آن = 'about/from that,' گمانی 'a suspicion/guess,' and نبرد is an archaic negative past form (ن + برد, "did not take/have"), so the clause means "that no one in the world had any suspicion of it."
segments.s722-g61: پس is a sentence adverb 'then/thereupon.' آن نامه پنهان is the noun phrase 'that hidden letter.' به خواهرش داد is a normal transitive clause: به + indirect object 'to his sister' (خواهر + enclitic ش 'his') and داد is the past tense 'gave.' Word order is Subject (he, implied) — Object — Verb (gave).
segments.s722-g62: سخنهای خاقان همه کرد یاد is a light-verb construction: سخنهای خاقان ('the khagan's words/sayings') + همه ('all') + کرد یاد (کرد + یاد forming 'to make remembrance' = 'to remind' or 'to recall'). The noun یاد follows کرد here to form the verbal idea 'reminded/recalled.'
segments.s722-g63: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of/about.' The series پیوند, پند, نیکوسخن are coordinated items (bond/kinship, counsel/advice, and 'good-speaking' i.e. eloquent or good words). وز here represents a compressed 'و ز' ('and from') connecting the second item; the whole phrase means 'from ties and from advice and eloquent speech.'
segments.s722-g64: The correlative چه ... چه functions as 'whether ... or ...' (or 'both ... and ...'). از نو = 'from new' meaning 'of recent times,' چه از روزگار کهن = 'or from the ancient age.' Word order is typical poetic repetition for emphasis and scope.
segments.s722-g81: مباد is an optative negative particle meaning 'may it not be'. ایچ here means 'nothing' / 'not at all' and combines with the following noun phrase گیتی ز خاقان تهی to express 'that the world be empty of the khagan'. ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of', and تهی is a predicative adjective 'empty / devoid (of)'. Word order is emphatic: negative wish + adverb + subject (گیتی + ز + خاقان) + predicate (تهی).
segments.s722-g82: بدو = به + او 'to him' (dative). شاد بادا is an optative clause 'may (it / he) be happy', where بادا marks the wish. کلاه مهی is a noun phrase 'moon‑like hat/crown' (کلاه modified by the adjective مهی); the adjective follows the noun in this poetic order. The whole segment gives a wish for the ruler: 'to him may the moon‑like crown be joyful.'
segments.s722-g83: کنون 'now'. چون here = 'when / now that' (introducing a temporal clause: 'now that we have sat'). نشستیم is the first‑person plural past 'we sat down' (the given lemma is irregularly listed; functionally it is past perfective here). با یکدگر 'with one another' follows as a complement, meaning we are seated together.
segments.s722-g84: بخوانیم is present/subjunctive first‑person plural used hortatively: 'let us read / let us recite'. نامه همه سر به سر is the object phrase: نامه 'book/epistle', همه 'whole/entire', and سر به سر is an idiomatic adverbial/adjunct 'from head to head' = 'throughout / from beginning to end'. The word order places the hortative verb before its object (typical in poetry).
segments.s722-g85: بدان is the prepositional phrase به + آن 'to that (one)'. کو is the relative/archaic particle 'who / which' linking to the relative clause. بزرگست is a third‑person singular predicate 'is great'. دارد خرد is a possession construction: دارد + noun = 'possesses wisdom' (دارد = 'has', خرد = 'wisdom'). Together: 'to that one who is great and has wisdom'.
segments.s722-g86: یکایک is an adverb 'one by one / each and every'. بدین is به + این 'to this', marking direction of the wish. آرزو 'desire / wish' is the object of the following verb. بنگرد is a third‑person singular present/subjunctive of نگریستن/نگرستن 'to look/turn toward / regard' (here: 'let each one direct / turn'). The clause means 'let each one direct this wish to that great and wise person.'
segments.s722-g87: کنون 'now'. دوده را 'the smoke' with را marking it as definite/topic. سر به سر is an idiom 'all over / entirely / one after another' modifying the predicate. شیونست = شیون + است 'is lamentation' (شیون = lament; -ست/-است third person copula). The clause states as a general observation: 'now (there) the smoke is all lamentation.'
segments.s722-g88: نه is the negation 'not'. هنگامه here means 'the critical moment / occasion' (a sense close to 'crisis / suitable time'). این سخن 'this speech/word'. گفتنست is a classical verblike predicate meaning 'is for saying / is the time to speak' (lit. 'is saying-to' — here used to say 'this is not the time to speak'). The whole clause: 'this is not the time/opportunity for this speech.'
segments.s722-g89: چو here is the temporal conjunction 'when'. سوک چنان مهتر is a noun phrase 'such a mourner/chief' (چنان = 'such/so', مهتر = 'chief/headman'). آید is present-tense 'comes'. به سر is a locative phrase 'to/at the head' (literally 'to the head'), placed after the verb in the classical word order.
segments.s722-g90: ز فرمان خاقان is an ablative/agent phrase 'by (or from) the khagan's order' (ز = 'by/from', فرمان = 'order', خاقان = 'khagan'). نباشد is the negative present/subjunctive form of 'to be' used impersonally here ('there is not / there will not be'). گذر means 'passage/passing'; the clause states that no passage is allowed except under the khagan's command.
segments.s722-g91: مرا خود (emphatic 'I myself') is the topical/accusative phrase; به ایران شدن is an infinitival/gerundial expression 'to go/become to Iran' (شدن used as an intransitive 'to go/set out' in older Persian). روی نیست is an idiom meaning 'there is no face/heart' — i.e. 'I have no inclination/ability/face' — with نیست negating existence in the present.
segments.s722-g92: زن پاک را is a topical/definite object 'a chaste/pure woman' (را marking definiteness/emphasis). به ز تو = 'because of you / on your account' (ز = 'from/because of', here giving cause). شوی is from شدن 'to become' (here 'become hers/yours'); combined with نیست the clause means 'there is no chaste woman who will become yours because of you' (negated existence/result).
segments.s722-g93: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. من is the subject 'I'. بدین زودی is an adverbial phrase 'so soon' (بدین = 'in this way/so', زودی = 'soon'). آیم is the present/subjunctive of آمدن 'to come' (here 'set out/arrive'); به راه is a locative 'on the road/route'. The clause is a conditional protasis.
segments.s722-g94: چه is the interrogative 'what'. گوید here functions as 'will say' (classical present/subjunctive/epic present used for future/quotative). مرا is the object 'to me' (mora = me), with را optionally marking emphasis/topic. آن خردمند شاه = 'that wise king' (آن = 'that'). Together the clause asks 'what will that wise king say to me?'.
segments.s722-g95: خردمند بی‌شرم is a noun phrase 'the wise (man) [shameless]' where بی‌شرم is an epithet 'shameless'. خواند is the simple past 'called/named' (he called), and مرا is the object 'me'. The clause therefore means 'the wise man called me shameless.'
segments.s722-g96: چو here means 'as'/'just as' (introducing a comparison). خاقان بی آزرم = 'the khagan without (a) modesty' (بی = 'without'); داند is the present of دانستن 'he knows/considers' and مرا را marks the object 'me'. The clause says that the khagan likewise 'considers' or 'deems' him so (i.e. shameless).
segments.s722-g97: بدین سوک چون بگذرد چار ماه is a temporal clause: بدین سوک ('in/with this mourning') sets the reference, then چون بگذرد ('when/after ... passes') introduces the time clause and uses بگذرد (3rd-person present/subjunctive) to mark a future/temporal event. چار ماه ('four months') is the time measure and appears after the verb in poetic word order.
segments.s722-g98: سواری فرستم به نزدیک شاه is the main clause: سواری (the object, 'a rider/horseman') is fronted, فرستم is 1st-person present/subjunctive used here with a future/volitional sense ('I will send'), and به نزدیک شاه is a prepositional phrase indicating the destination ('to/near the king').
segments.s722-g99: همه بشنوم هرچ باید شنید: همه ('everything/all') is the thing to be heard and بشنوم is 1st-person present/subjunctive/optative ('may I hear' / 'I will hear') expressing the speaker's intention. هرچ باید شنید is a subordinate nominal clause modifying 'همه' — هرچ ('that which/whatever') + باید ('it is necessary') + شنید (infinitive/nominal form 'to be heard'), meaning 'whatever must be heard.'
segments.s722-g100: بگویندگان تا چه آید پدید: بگویندگان ('the tellers/narrators') names the source; تا introduces a purpose/result clause here ('so that' / 'in order that'), and چه آید پدید is a fused interrogative/declarative nominal meaning 'what will come to light/occur' (آید پدید = 'come to pass/appear').
segments.s722-g101: بگویم یکایک به نامه درون: بگویم (here used as a first-person verb 'I will say/write/record') expresses the speaker's intention; یکایک ('one by one') is an adverbial/quantifier modifying the action; به نامه درون is a prepositional phrase 'into/within a letter' (نامه = letter, درون = inside/within).
segments.s722-g102: چو آید به نزدیک او رهنمون is a temporal clause: چو ('when') + آید ('comes', 3rd-person present/subjunctive used temporally) introduces the time; به نزدیک او ('to/near him') marks the locus, and رهنمون ('guide') is the noun that 'comes' — together 'when a guide comes near him.'
segments.s722-g103: تو اکنون از ایدر به شادی خرام: تو ('you') is the addressee; اکنون ('now') gives time; از ایدر ('from here') is the origin; به شادی ('with joy') is an adverbial phrase; خرام is an imperative/hortative verb form meaning 'stride/prance/go proudly' directed to 'تو' (imperative sense 'go with joyful stride').
segments.s722-g104: به خاقان بگو آنچ دادم پیام: به خاقان بگو is an imperative phrase 'tell the khagan'; آنچ ('that which') introduces the content clause, and دادم (past 1st person 'I gave') together with پیام ('message') forms the object 'the message that I gave.' Word order places the relative 'آنچ' and verb 'دادم' before the noun پیام for poetic emphasis.
segments.s722-g33: This is a pair of parallel nominal phrases joined by و (and). همه here functions as an intensifier meaning “whole/entire” before the noun (i.e. “complete honesty”); راستی is a noun meaning uprightness/truthfulness. The second همه modifies مردمی in the same way (“entirely of the people” / “wholly popular”), and the whole phrase is an attributive cluster that will attach to سرشتت (“your disposition”).
segments.s722-g34: سرشتت is سرشت + enclitic ت (your nature/disposition) and is the grammatical subject. فزونی is a nominal predicate meaning abundance/increase. و links the two predicate qualities; دور از کمی is a prepositional phrase ('far from lack') with دور (distant/far) + از (from) + کمی (scarcity). The whole segment states the predicate about سرشتت: “your disposition is abundant and far from scarcity.”
segments.s722-g35: ز here is the poetic preposition meaning “because of/from,” taking the following noun as cause. کارِ تو (“your deed/affair”) is the object of the causal preposition. اندیشه کردن is expressed with اندیشه + کردم (past of کردن), so اندیشه کردم دراز = “I thought/pondered for a long time.” Word order is somewhat compressed for poetry: cause (ز کار تو) + verb (اندیشه کردم) + adverb (دراز).
segments.s722-g36: نشسته is a past-participle/adjectival verb meaning “having sat/settled” and modifies the following noun خرد (“wisdom”) to mean “wisdom that has settled.” با دلِ من is a prepositional phrase “with/in my heart” (با used poetically for accompaniment/possession), and براز is a final noun (here used metaphorically in the line) — literally “manure” in the lexical list; the phrase therefore reads literally “wisdom, settled with my heart, (as) b[rarz].”
segments.s722-g37: The comparative construction به از تو literally places به before از + pronoun to mean “better than you” (poetic order). ندیدم is the past negative “I did not see”; کسی (“anyone”) is the indefinite noun object/predicate. کدخدای is in apposition/predicate position — “anyone (to act as) a kadhkhudā (steward/headman).” Word order is inverted for poetic effect: comparative phrase + verb + indefinite + apposition.
segments.s722-g38: ایوان ما را is the definite object (our iwan/portico, with را marking the object). به رای is an instrumental/means phrase “by (your) counsel/opinion” (به + رای). بیارای functions here as a verbal/inflected form meaning “to adorn/ornament” in an archaic/poetic shape; syntactically it governs the object ایوان ما را and takes به رای to indicate how it is adorned. (Reading: “to adorn our hall with counsel.”)
segments.s722-g39: بدارم is a present-subjunctive/volitional form of داشتن used here as a volitional ‘I will hold/keep’; it expresses the speaker’s resolve. تو را is the direct object marked with را. همچو جان is a simile/adverbial phrase “as (my) life” (همچو = “like/as” + جان = “life”), and و تنم appends “and my body” (تن + enclitic -م “my”). The whole segment says the speaker will hold the addressee as dear as his life and body.
segments.s722-g40: بکوشم is the present-subjunctive/optative of کوشیدن (“I will strive / I will try”). که introduces a subordinate clause ‘that/in order that’ and here introduces the content of the striving. پیمان تو is the object (your pledge/covenant). نشکنم is the negative present-subjunctive of شکستَن (ن + شکنم) meaning “that I do not break” (i.e. ‘I will strive not to break your pledge’).
segments.s722-g41: وزان پس is an archaic/poetic sequence (و + ز آن پس) meaning “and after that / from that time on.” بدین is composed of the preposition بِ (to/into/for) + the demonstrative (here ‘this’), so بدین شهر = “of/over this city.” فرمان تراست places the copular verb تراست (= ترا + است) at the end: ترا = “to you,” است = “is,” so “the command is yours.”
segments.s722-g42: گروگان کنم is a first‑person expression with کنم (here ‘I will/may make’ or ‘I will cause’); گروگان is the noun object meaning ‘hostage/pledge.’ دل is the direct object (‘the heart’). بدانچت is a compound/contracted phrase meaning roughly ‘because of that / toward you on account of that’ and هواست is composed of هوا (desire/mood) + ست (copula ‘is’): ‘my heart’s desire is (so).’ The verb-state is expressed by ست at the end.
segments.s722-g43: کنون is an adverb ‘now.’ هرکه داری is a relative clause ‘whoever/whomever you have’ with the verb داری (you have) after the relative. همه گرد کن is an imperative: همه = ‘all/everyone,’ گرد کن uses گرد as a verbal/adverbial root meaning ‘gather/bring together’ plus کن (imperative ‘do’), so ‘gather all.’
segments.s722-g44: به پیش is a prepositional phrase ‘to/in the presence of / before.’ خردمند is the addressee/target noun ‘the wise (one).’ گوی is an imperative/archaic verbal form related to گفتن (‘say’) used here with این سخن ‘these words’ as its object — so ‘say these words before the wise man.’ Word order places the verb (گوی) before the noun phrase (این سخن).
segments.s722-g45: ازین پس is a temporal phrase ‘from now on / henceforth’ (از + این + پس). ببین is an imperative ‘see/observe.’ تاچه is an interrogative/adverbial ‘what/ how much,’ and آیدت is a verbal form ‘comes to you’ (آید + enclitic ت = ‘to you’). رای is the object meaning ‘counsel/plan/opinion’ — so the clause asks you to observe what counsel comes to you.
segments.s722-g46: به روشن روانت is a prepositional phrase ‘to/for your enlightened/clear soul’ (به + روشن as adjective + روان + enclitic ت ‘your’). خرد رهنمای is a verbless nominal clause in which خرد ‘wisdom’ is predicated as رهنمای ‘guide’ (the copula is omitted): literally ‘wisdom [be] guide.’ The construction therefore means ‘let wisdom be the guide of your enlightened soul.’
segments.s722-g47: خرد را uses the object marker را to mark خرد (‘wisdom’) as the definite object. بران is a compressed form of بر آن (‘over/on that/those’), so بران مردمان = ‘over those people.’ شاه کن is an imperative: شاه (king) is the predicate-noun and کن is the imperative ‘make’ — ‘make wisdom king over those people.’
segments.s722-g48: مرا زآن is the object clause starting with مرا ‘me’ (here split in the word list into م + را) plus زآن ‘about/from that.’ سگالیده is a participial adjective meaning something like ‘set upon / attacked / worried (by dogs)’ and is modified by زآن; آگاه کن is an imperative phrase meaning ‘make [me] aware’ = ‘inform me.’ Altogether: ‘Inform me about that which was set upon/attacked’ (i.e. tell me about that incident).
segments.s722-g73: بدو گفت introduces reported speech: بدو = 'to him' (prepositional pronoun) and گفت = 'said' (3rd‑person past). کاین מנה بر خواندم is the subordinate clause introduced by ک(=که) meaning 'that'; برخواندم is 1st‑person past (bar‑خواندم) meaning 'I read/recited' (the prefix بر often implies reading aloud or completion). Word order here is typical poetic Persian: indirect object (بدو) + reporting verb (گفت) + که + reported clause with its own subject/verb.
segments.s722-g74: خرد را بر خویش بنشاندم is literal 'I placed/seated wisdom upon myself.' رابر is the fused sequence را + بر (object marker + preposition 'upon'); خویش is the reflexive 'one's own' (here written as خوی + ش), and بنشاندم is 1st‑person past 'I caused to sit / I set'. The clause is coordinated with the previous reported clause as part of what he 'said'.
segments.s722-g75: چنان کرد = 'did thus/so acted' (چنان 'so', کرد past of کردن). خاقان is the subject following the verb (normal in poetic word order). که introduces the result/purpose clause 'so that / that', and شاهان کنند is a generic/habitual present/subjunctive formulation ('kings do' / 'kings should act'), where کنند is 3rd‑person plural present/subjunctive used to express a general habit or expected behavior.
segments.s722-g76: This continues the content of what kings are to do: جهاندیده is an adjectival/epithet 'worldly‑wise / world‑seer', و = 'and', پیشگاهان here refers to 'the presence / the court (attending the throne)', and کنند (the same verb) applies to both coordinated predicates (i.e., 'be worldly‑wise and attend the court'). The verb is understood to govern both coordinated elements.
segments.s722-g77: بدو + باد forms an optative wish: بدو = 'to him', باد = 'may it be' (optative particle). روشن جهان‑بین من is a noun phrase 'my bright/clear world‑seer' (روشن qualifying جهان‑بین, followed by the possessor من). The whole phrase means 'May my clear‑sighted/world‑seer be (a boon) to him.'
segments.s722-g78: که introduces a purposive/result clause 'so that'; چونین = 'thus/so', بجوید is 3rd‑person singular present/subjunctive 'may seek' or 'seeks' (here optative/desired result), همی is an emphatic/continuative particle ('ever/always' or 'indeed' in poetry), and کین من = 'my vengeance/hatred' (object of بجوید). Word order places object after the verb in this poetic clause.
segments.s722-g79: دل او = 'his heart'. ز (poetic) = 'from / because of'; تیمار = 'care, solicitude'; خسته = 'weary'; مباد = negative optative 'may it not be'. Together they form the wish 'May his heart not become weary from care.' Note the optative particle مباد negates the predicate.
segments.s722-g80: امید جهان = 'the hope of the world' or 'hope in the world'; زو is the contracted poetic form meaning 'from him/it' (ز + او), گسسته = 'broken/severed', مباد = 'may it not be' (negative optative). The clause therefore says 'May the world's hope not be severed from him.'
segments.s722-g65: This is a short noun phrase introduced by the poetic preposition ز (z = 'from/of'), listing two abstract qualities: پاکی 'purity/chastity' and (with a second preposition از) پارسایی 'piety/uprightness'. The final noun زن (woman) is the head: ز/از ... زن = 'from/of the woman's ...'. Word order is preposition + qualities + head noun, typical of Persian poetic compression.
segments.s722-g66: که begins a relative clause modifying زن ('who/that'). هم ... هم marks a two-part balanced predication: هم غمگسارست 'is also a sympathizer' و هم رای زن 'and also of counsel/has good counsel' — غمگسارست is a fused adjective+copula (consoler/is sympathetic), رای followed by the repeated noun زن echoes the head noun for emphasis ('a woman of counsel').
segments.s722-g67: This is two coordinated clauses: 'جوان گفت' = 'the young man spoke/said' (subject + verb) followed by 'و' + 'آن پاکدامن شنید' = 'and that chaste one heard'. آن 'that' signals the previously-mentioned chaste person; پاکدامن is an adjectival noun 'chaste/upright'.
segments.s722-g68: ز گفتار او is an ablative/causal phrase 'from/on account of his speech' (ز = 'from/of/upon'), followed by خامشی برگزید: خامشی 'silence' is the object of برگزید 'chose/selected'. The past tense verb برگزید closes the clause and gives the action resulting from hearing.
segments.s722-g69: وزان پس is a temporal connective 'and/after that' (وزان = 'and from that', پس = 'then/after'). چو here means 'when', and برخواند is the past/future-poetic form 'read aloud/recited'. آن نامه را uses the object marker را to mark 'that letter' as the definite object of reading.
segments.s722-g70: This is a noun phrase functioning as the object/continuation of what was read: سخنهای 'the words/speeches' of خاقان 'the khagan' with خود 'own' (emphatic/possessive) and کامه qualifying those words as 'pleasing/desired'. The trailing را again marks the whole phrase as a definite object.
segments.s722-g71: خرد را is the theme/topic marked by را ('wisdom' as the thing in question). چو = 'when', با دانش = 'with knowledge' (instrumental phrase), and the verb phrase انباز کرد is archaic/obscure (انباز is labeled obsolete) with کرد as the past auxiliary 'did/caused' — together roughly 'he tested/unfolded/examined wisdom using knowledge'.
segments.s722-g72: به دل is a dative/locative phrase 'in/into the heart', پاسخ‌نامه را is the definite object 'the reply-letter', and ساز کرد is a light-verb construction 'made/constructed' (ساز as 'arrangement/means' + کرد 'made'), so the whole means 'he formed/composed the reply in his heart'.
segments.s722-g17: که introduces a subordinate clause meaning 'that' or 'so that'. کسهای is a plural noun 'people/some persons' (here 'people'); بهرام یل is a name + epithet 'Bahram the hero'. را is the Persian definite/object marker attached to یل, marking یل as the direct object. ببین is an exhortative/subjunctive/present-form of دیدن here meaning 'may (they) see' or 'let (people) see' and links the clause as the reason/purpose for what follows.
segments.s722-g18: فراوان is an adverb modifying the verb, 'plentifully' or 'lavishly'. برای + شان functions together as 'for them' (برای = 'for', شان = 'their/them'). بخواند is a 3rd-person subjunctive/jussive/present form of خواندن meaning 'may he recite/read (praise)'. آفرین is a noun/exclamation 'praise, acclamation' — the object being recited.
segments.s722-g19: بگو is the imperative 'say' (addressing someone to report what follows). آنک is an archaic connective meaning 'that' or 'for that (which)', introducing the reported clause. من خود places emphasis on 'I myself'. جگر خسته‌ام is a verbless or copular predicate with the enclitic -ام: جگر here means 'heart/seat of feeling' (poetic for sorrow), and خسته‌ام = 'I am weary'.
segments.s722-g20: بدین سوک is a prepositional phrase 'to/into this mourning' (بدین = 'to/into this', سوک = 'mourning/dirge'). تا زنده‌ام is a temporal subordinate clause 'until I am alive' with the enclitic -ام on زنده. بسته‌ام is a perfective/present perfect-like verb 'I have bound/fastened' (here 'I have bound/committed myself'), so the clause means 'I have bound myself to this mourning as long as I live.'
segments.s722-g21: به خون روی کشور بشستم is a verbal clause: به خون ('with blood' or 'in blood') modifies بشستم ('I washed/cleansed'). روی کشور = 'the face/surface of the realm' (روی = face/surface, کشور = country/realm) is the object of بشستم. ز کین ('out of/from vengeance') gives the motive: ز = 'from/because of', کین = 'venge/hatred'. بشستم is first-person past 'I washed'.
segments.s722-g22: همه شهر is the subject phrase 'the whole city' or 'everyone in the city' (همه = all/everyone, شهر = city). نفرین بدو is a verbal phrase 'cursed him' where نفرین is 'curse' (used as a noun/verb here) and بدو = 'to him'. آفرین at the end is an exclamatory noun 'curse/accusation' or an intensifying exclamation (here functioning as the cry or echo of the curse).
segments.s722-g23: بدین درد = 'by/with this pain/grief' (بدین = 'by/with this', درد = 'affliction/sorrow'). هر چند here means 'however many/however much' and qualifies کین آورم: کین آورم is a subjunctive/optative or present-future 'I bring about/exact vengeance' (آورم = 'I cause/bring about'). The clause expresses that, under this grief, he will inflict vengeance in whatever measure.
segments.s722-g24: وگر is a conditional 'and if / but if'. آسمان بر زمین آورم is a hyperbolic conditional: آسمان = 'sky/heaven', بر = 'upon/to/against' and زمین = 'earth'; آورم here is 'I would bring' (آورد#آور). Together the clause means 'if so, I would bring the heavens down upon the earth' — an image for extreme destructive power or vengeance.
segments.s722-g49: همی‌رفت is a past habitual/simple-past poetic form ('was going' / 'kept going'). برسان is an infinitival/purpose-form here ('to bring, to deliver'), expressing the dove's purpose. قمری is the subject (the turtledove) placed after the verb (poetic inversion). ز سرو is a prepositional phrase 'from the cypress' indicating origin.
segments.s722-g50: بیامد is simple past 'came'. برادرش is the subject + possessive suffix 'his brother'. تازان here is a postpositive adjective/epithet modifying برادرش ('fierce'); بہ مرو is the destination prepositional phrase ('to Merv'). Word order is verb–subject–modifier–prepositional phrase, a common poetic arrangement.
segments.s722-g51: جهانجوی is the nominal subject 'the world-seeker / ambitious man'. با + نامور indicates the agent or instrument ('by/with the renowned one'). رام شد is a verbal-complement phrase meaning 'became tamed' (شد = change of state auxiliary). The whole segment reads 'the world-seeker was tamed by the famed.'
segments.s722-g52: به نزدیک is a prepositional phrase meaning 'to/into the nearness (i.e. into the presence)'. کسهای (plural/collective) functions as 'people/persons' and is governed by that preposition; بهرام is the possessor ('of Bahram'). شد again is 'became / came to be'—here 'he came near to Bahram's people.'
segments.s722-g53: بگفت (here 'told/related') introduces reported speech. آنچ is the relative 'that which / what' and functions as the object of بگفت. خاقان is the agent/author of the earlier speech; بدو 'to him' is the indirect object. گفته بود is pluperfect 'had said' (گفته = past participle, بود used as auxiliary), so the clause: 'he said what the Khaghan had told him.'
segments.s722-g54: که introduces a subordinate content clause 'that ...'. از کین indicates cause/reason 'because of / from the hatred/vengeance'. آن کشته is a genitival phrase 'that slain one' (کشته used as substantive 'the slain'). آشفته بود is simple past 'was disturbed / upset'. Together: 'that he was upset because of the vengeance of that slain (man).'
segments.s722-g55: ازان پس is a temporal/discourse phrase 'after that / thereupon'. چنین گفت is a formulaic 'thus he said' (چنین = 'thus', گفت = 'said' / 'speech' in poetic diction). کای is the vocative particle 'O', and بخردان is the vocative plural 'wise ones' (ب- prefix + خردان). The whole segment is the speech preface: 'Thereupon he said: O wise ones...'.
segments.s722-g56: پسندیده and کار دیده are postpositive adjectival/participial epithets modifying the addressee. پسندیده 'chosen/approved'; و is the coordinating 'and'; کار دیده is a compound where دیده is a past participle 'seen' used idiomatically with کار ('affair') to mean 'experienced in affairs'. ردان is a short poetic epithet following those modifiers (postpositive epithet). Together the phrase addresses the audience as 'chosen and experienced-in-affairs (elders/judges)'.
segments.s722-g9: نکوهش کند is a verb phrase with نکوهش (reproach) as object/noun and کند the present-subjunctive/optative auxiliary 'may/let (him) do'. The following relative clause هرک این بشنود ('whoever hears this') specifies the person: هرک = 'whoever' (relative), این = demonstrative 'this' referring to the foregoing, and بشنود is a 3rd-person present/subjunctive ('hears'/'may hear') that ties to the jussive sense of کند.
segments.s722-g10: ازین پس is an adverbial phrase ('from now on' / 'henceforth') formed from از + این + پس; به سوگند من نگرود contains به as the preposition 'by'/'by means of', سوگند من 'my oath' (من functioning as the possessor), and نگرود a negative jussive/subjunctive 3rd-person singular (ن‑ prefix + گروید/گرویدن) meaning 'let him not adopt/accept'. Altogether: 'henceforth, let him not be bound by my oath.'
segments.s722-g11: نخوردم is first-person past with negative prefix (ن‑) from خوردن used idiomatically ('to feel/undergo sorrow' as in غم خوردن), so 'I did not grieve'. غم is the direct object 'sorrow', and خرد فرزند اوی = 'his little/young son' where خرد functions as the adjective 'small/young' before فرزند and اوی is an older possessive form 'his'.
segments.s722-g12: نه begins a second negative clause 'nor'; اندیشه خویش is 'his own thought/planning'—here خوی + ش are split orthographically to form خویش (possessive 'own/his'), and پیوند اوی is 'his bond/alliance' with اوی the possessive. The clause omits an explicit verb (understood repetition of 'did not grieve'), a common poetic ellipsis.
segments.s722-g13: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when'; با ما is the prepositional phrase 'with us'; به فرزند indicates the role/relationship ('as a son' or 'to a son') formed with به + noun; پیوسته شد is the verbal complex (پیوسته past participle 'joined/connected' + auxiliary شد 'became') expressing 'became joined'. The verb appears at the end, as typical in Persian.
segments.s722-g14: به مهر و خرد is an instrumental phrase 'by/through love and wisdom' (به used here to mark the instrument/means); جان او is the patient 'his soul/life' and شسته شد is a passive construction (شسته past participle 'washed/cleansed' + شد auxiliary) meaning 'was cleansed.' Word order places the instruments before the affected noun and the verbal complex at the end in typical poetic diction.
segments.s722-g15: بفرمود is an honorific/polite past verb 'he said/commanded' (formal register). تا introduces a subordinate temporal clause 'until/when', and شد is the clause-final past auxiliary 'came/was', so تا شد برادرش پیش = 'until/when his brother came forward (before him)'; برادرش is 'his brother' (برادر + ش possessive) and پیش is adverbial 'before/forward/in presence'.
segments.s722-g16: سخن گفت is the compound 'spoke/talked' (سخن 'speech' + گفت here functioning as 'spoke'); با او 'with him' marks the interlocutor. ز اندازه بیش is an adverbial phrase meaning 'beyond measure' or 'more than proper' (ز = 'from/of' used poetically, اندازه = 'measure/degree', بیش = 'more/excess'), qualifying the manner or excess of his speech.
segments.s722-g25: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/by'; فرمان یزدان is a genitive phrase 'the command of God'. کسی is an indefinite pronoun placed after the prepositional phrase (poetic word order) and here functions negatively ('no one'). نگذرد is 3rd-person singular present/optative of گذشتن used impersonally to mean 'does not/cannot pass' (poetic present expressing general impossibility).
segments.s722-g26: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so' introducing a comment. داند is the archaic/poetic 3rd-person present of دانستن ('knows'); it precedes the subject in inverted word order. آنکس که دارد خرد is a relative construction 'the one who has wisdom' where دارد is present 'has' and خرد is the substantive 'wisdom'.
segments.s722-g27: که here introduces an explanatory clause linked to the previous statement. او را uses را to mark the affected person (dative/topic marking 'for him'). زمانه is the subject meaning 'the times/fortune', بران گونه is a locative/manner phrase 'in/of that sort', and بود is the past copula 'was'. The order is slightly archaic/poetic.
segments.s722-g28: همه is the subject 'all/everyone'. The adjectives/nominals تنبل and دیو وارونه describe them: تنبل 'lazy', دیو used figuratively 'devilish/fiend-like', وارونه 'contrary/perverse'. بود is the past copula 'were'. The phrase lists qualities of the people (poetic piling of epithets).
segments.s722-g29: بران 'thereupon/therefore' starts the sentence. زینهارم is first-person admonition formed from زینهار with -م 'I warn' or 'my warning' (here a verbal/admonitory sense). که introduces the content clause 'that ...', گفتم is the simple past 'I said/spoke' (poetic past), and سخن is the object 'word(s)/speech'.
segments.s722-g30: بران again 'thereupon'. عهد و پیمان is a compound noun 'oath and covenant' (direct object). نهادیم is 1st-person plural past of نهادن 'we placed/laid (down)'. بن is a noun 'foundation/base' used adverbially to indicate that the oath/covenant was set as a basis.
segments.s722-g31: سوی is the preposition 'toward/to'. گردیه here functions as 'assembled/gathered' (either participle or a noun 'assembly'), so سوی گردیه marks the direction/target. نامه‌ای is an indefinite noun 'a letter' (the -ای indefinite), and بد جدا are adjectival modifiers 'evil/bad' and 'separate/separately' describing the letter. Word order puts the directional phrase first, followed by the described noun phrase.
segments.s722-g32: که‌ای (literary vocative structure 'که ای' contracted here) introduces direct address quoted in the letter: it functions as an archaic/vocative 'O'. پاکدامن and پارسا are epithets 'chaste' and 'pious', with زن 'woman' between them; the three together form the vocative phrase 'O chaste, pious woman'.
segments.s722-g1: ازآن پس is an adverbial phrase 'from that time / thereafter'. چو here means 'when' and introduces a subordinate temporal clause. خاقان is the subject 'the khaghan'. به + پردخت uses به with a verbal noun (پردخت = پرداخت 'payment/act of paying/avenging') and دل ('heart') as its complement: 'with/inthe paying of the heart' (i.e. when the khaghan set his heart/resolve). Word order is literary/poetic (temporal phrase + چو + subject + verbal-noun phrase).
segments.s722-g2: ز خون is a prepositional phrase 'from/with blood' (poetic z). شد is the past of شدن 'became'. همه کشور چین is the subject 'the whole realm/country of China' (کشور چین = 'the land/realm of China'). چوگل is a simile 'like a flower' (چو = like, گل = flower), applied here to the state of the land. The clause thus says the realm was turned, by blood, into something like a flower (a poetic image).
segments.s722-g3: چنین گفت یک روز is a common narrative frame 'one day he said thus' (چنین 'thus/so' + گفت 'said' + یک روز 'one day' as a temporal phrase). کز is the contracted form of که + از / relative 'that/from which' introducing the content clause: 'that from a man who is سست (weak)...'. The adjective سست modifies مرد 'man'.
segments.s722-g4: نیاید is a negative present/past-subjunctive sense 'does not come / does not arise'. مگر here means 'except / unless'. کار نا تندرست is a noun phrase 'an unhealthy / ill (نا + تندرست) affair/deed'—نا is the negative prefix attached to تندرست. The whole clause: 'nothing comes except an unhealthy deed.'
segments.s722-g5: بدان here is the fused literary prepositional/adverbial 'to/at that' (written as ب + دان in the segmentation). نامداری is the noun 'renown / famed person'. که introduces the relative clause 'who was Bahram' (بهرام بود = 'was Bahram'). بود is the past copula 'was'. The structure identifies the famed person as Bahram.
segments.s722-g6: مرا زو is the object-expression 'to me / for me from him' (مرا = me + object marker را; ز (از) + او = 'from him' contracted as زو). همه رامش و کام بود is a predicative phrase: 'all (همه) peace/comfort (رامش) and fulfilment/desire (کام) there was' — بود functions as the past of 'to be' and here yields the sense 'I had from him all peace and satisfaction.' Note the literary order (object before verb).
segments.s722-g7: کنون من is 'now I'. ز کسهای آن نامدار uses ز (from/of) + کسهای (plural/collective 'persons/people') + آن نامدار 'that renowned (group)' — literally 'I, from the persons of that famed (lineage/group)'. This expresses membership/association: 'I, belonging to those illustrious ones.'
segments.s722-g8: چرا is the interrogative 'why'. بازماندم is the past form 'I remained / I was left behind'. چنین is an intensifier 'so / thus'. سست و خوار are predicates 'weak and despised/lowly'. The clause asks why the speaker, belonging to a renowned group, remains in such a weak and humbled state.

sentences.s722-s53: He gave the envoy many gifts; the worldly‑wise envoy returned from Merv rejoicing.
sentences.s722-s29: May this great reward be yours, and may the just judge be his ally.
sentences.s722-s30: Suddenly there was a death — that was no matter of wisdom — so that no one in the world had any suspicion of it.
sentences.s722-s31: Then he gave that hidden letter to his sister and reminded her of all the khagan's words.
sentences.s722-s32: — about ties and about counsel and eloquent words, whether of recent times or of ancient days.
sentences.s722-s41: May the world never be empty of a khagan; may a moon‑like crown bring him joy.
sentences.s722-s42: Now that we have sat together, let us read the whole book from beginning to end.
sentences.s722-s43: Let each one turn this wish toward that one who is great and possesses wisdom.
sentences.s722-s44: Now the smoke is all lamentation; this is not the moment for such speech.
sentences.s722-s45: When such a chief mourner comes forward at the head, there will be no passing except by the khagan's command.
sentences.s722-s46: I myself have no wish/face to go to Iran; a chaste woman will not become yours on your account.
sentences.s722-s47: If I set out on the road so soon, what will that wise king say to me?
sentences.s722-s48: The wise man called me shameless, even as the khagan, lacking modesty, judges me so.
sentences.s722-s49: When four months of this mourning have passed, I will send a rider to the king's presence.
sentences.s722-s50: I will hear everything that ought to be heard from the narrators, so that I may see what will come to pass.
sentences.s722-s51: I will write each one in a letter within, when a guide comes near him.
sentences.s722-s52: You now, from here, go forth with joyful stride; tell the khagan the message that I gave.
sentences.s722-s17: Your disposition is all honesty and entirely of the people; it is abundant and far from lack.
sentences.s722-s18: Because of your deed I pondered at length; wisdom sat (and) with my heart as manure.
sentences.s722-s19: I have seen no one better than you as a steward (kadhkhudā) to adorn our iwan with counsel.
sentences.s722-s20: I will hold you as my very life and body, and I will strive that I not break your pledge.
sentences.s722-s21: And after that the command of this city is yours; I will make my heart a hostage to you — my heart is inclined toward you because of that.
sentences.s722-s22: Now, gather together everyone you have; bring them before the wise man and say these words.
sentences.s722-s23: From now on, see what counsel comes to you; let wisdom be the guide of your enlightened soul.
sentences.s722-s24: Make wisdom king over those people; inform me about that which set (dogs) upon them / tell me of that attack.
sentences.s722-s37: He said to him that I have read this letter and set wisdom upon myself.
sentences.s722-s38: The khaghan did thus, so that kings should act — be worldly‑wise and attend the court.
sentences.s722-s39: May my clear, world‑seer be unto him; so that he may thus ever seek my vengeance.
sentences.s722-s40: May his heart not be wearied by care; may the world's hope not be severed from him.
sentences.s722-s33: Because of a woman's purity and piety — a woman who is both a sympathizer and sound in counsel.
sentences.s722-s34: The young man spoke, and that chaste one listened; on hearing his words she chose silence.
sentences.s722-s35: And after that, when he read that letter aloud, he set forth the khagan's own pleasing words.
sentences.s722-s36: When, with learning, he tried out wisdom, he formed the response-letter in his heart.
sentences.s722-s9: That people may see Bahram the hero, may he lavishly recite praises for them.
sentences.s722-s10: Say that I myself am weary at heart; I have pledged myself to this mourning while I live.
sentences.s722-s11: Out of vengeance I washed the face of the realm with blood; the whole city hurled curses at him.
sentences.s722-s12: By this grief, however much vengeance I may exact — and if need be, I would bring the sky down to the earth.
sentences.s722-s25: The turtledove flew off from the cypress to carry (the message); his brother, fierce, came to Merv.
sentences.s722-s26: The world-seeker was tamed by the renowned one, and he drew near to Bahram's people.
sentences.s722-s27: He related what the Khaghan had told him, namely that he was disturbed by the vengeance of that slain man.
sentences.s722-s28: Thereupon he said thus: 'O wise ones, chosen and experienced in affairs,'
sentences.s722-s5: May whoever hears this reproach him; henceforth let him not take my oath.
sentences.s722-s6: I did not grieve for his little son, nor for his own thoughts and alliances.
sentences.s722-s7: When he joined us as a son, by love and wisdom his soul was cleansed.
sentences.s722-s8: He ordered, and when his brother came forward he spoke with him beyond measure.
sentences.s722-s13: No one can pass beyond the command of God; this is known by the one who possesses wisdom.
sentences.s722-s14: For him the times were of that sort; everyone was lazy, devilish, and perverse.
sentences.s722-s15: Therefore I warn: I have spoken the words; upon that we laid an oath and covenant as a foundation.
sentences.s722-s16: He sent a separate evil letter to the assembly, saying, 'O chaste, pious woman!'
sentences.s722-s1: Thereafter, when the khaghan set his heart to striking, the whole realm of China, by blood, became like a flower.
sentences.s722-s2: One day he said thus: that from a weak man nothing comes except an unhealthy deed.
sentences.s722-s3: He was that famed one, Bahram; from him I had all peace and fulfilment.
sentences.s722-s4: Now why have I, of those famed men, been left behind so weak and despised?
